sources.text:
I came out to my mom as trans nonbinary at 34. 
She asked about going on a family trip in 2027 where I may need my passport and I said “well I’d have to surrender my X License and Birth certificate to even get a passport now” and she said “oh, why does it say X” 
“Because I had it changed to reflect who I am”
“I guess this is how I find out who you are”
🤣☠️ ✨

EDIT: I understand some of you do not understand my choice to discuss this publicly or my tone towards my mother but do understand we have a unique and sensitive relationship that is very okay with us calling each other out on things openly.

Why does this matter on LinkedIn?
Jobs require a gender to apply. 
My license and birth certificate say X. 
With the EO stating there are no Trans or Nonbinary people, this issue will impact my ability to work should I choose to look for a new role. I can't even update my license or passport because they would require that I turn in my X ones to obtain them. That is why I discuss this here, because there are other Trans and Nonbinary people experiencing this too, and I want to speak up for a part of our community.
imageDescriptions: [Image: No alternative text description for this image]
directClaims: I came out to my mom as trans nonbinary at 34
I had my license changed to X to reflect who I am
Jobs require a gender to apply
My license and birth certificate say X
There is an Executive Order stating there are no Trans or Nonbinary people
I cannot update my license or passport because they would require me to turn in my X ones to obtain them
This issue will impact my ability to work should I choose to look for a new role
inferredBeliefs: X gender markers are a meaningful reflection of identity
Government policy changes threaten transgender and nonbinary people's employment opportunities
Personal identity discussions belong in professional spaces when they affect workplace outcomes
Vulnerability and humor can coexist in serious conversations
Individual stories illustrate systemic barriers affecting vulnerable communities
sources.summary: Ty Garcia shares their experience coming out as trans nonbinary and explains how recent executive orders and document policies create barriers to employment and legal documentation updates for trans and nonbinary people.

sources.author: Aishwarya Naresh Reganti
sources.text:
🥳 🥳 Building AI agents? This is THE most comprehensive breakdown of the AI agent stack, covering key layers and the most popular open-source and closed-source options.

Each tool comes with standout features to help you decide what fits your needs. Here are the layers:
⛳ Model Serving & Inference – Defines how AI agents process inputs and generate outputs, balancing speed, cost, and control.
⛳ Storage Solutions – Ensures agents can store, retrieve, and manage data efficiently to maintain context and improve over time.
⛳ Tool Execution & Memory Management – Enables interaction with external systems, execution of commands, and retention of long-term memory.
⛳ AI Agent Development Frameworks – Provides structured environments for designing, scaling, and optimizing AI agents.
⛳ Observability & Monitoring – Tracks agent performance, detects errors, and ensures efficiency through real-time insights and debugging tools.

Link to the article: https://lnkd.in/eHkXWBuU
imageDescriptions: [Image: diagram]
directClaims: AI agent stack covers five key layers: Model Serving & Inference, Storage Solutions, Tool Execution & Memory Management, AI Agent Development Frameworks, and Observability & Monitoring
Model Serving & Inference balances speed, cost, and control
Storage Solutions enable agents to maintain context and improve over time
Tool Execution & Memory Management enables interaction with external systems and retention of long-term memory
Observability & Monitoring tracks agent performance and detects errors through real-time insights
inferredBeliefs: Understanding the AI agent stack architecture is essential for building effective AI agents
There are both open-source and closed-source options available for each layer
Tool selection should be driven by specific use-case needs rather than one-size-fits-all approach
Comprehensive breakdowns and comparisons help practitioners make informed decisions
sources.summary: A comprehensive breakdown of the AI agent technology stack across five key layers (model serving, storage, tool execution, frameworks, and observability), highlighting both open-source and closed-source options to help practitioners build effective AI agents.

sources.author: Tom Booker
sources.text:
What's your main purpose for being on LinkedIn?

I've been thinking about why I'm on LinkedIn, and here's what I've come up with: 
I want to find work as a scriptwriter and connect with people who might be interested in my using the principles of improv to improve their team's customer service.

So that's me! I'd love to know why you're here and how I can help! What's your reason for using LinkedIn right now?

Tom Booker wants to connect with people interested in using improv principles to improve team customer service
inferredBeliefs: Improv principles can enhance customer service training
LinkedIn is useful for finding freelance/contract work
LinkedIn is a platform for professional networking around specific skills

sources.author: Lara Acosta
sources.text:
I've made $100k+ in 4 days. The secret? My newsletter.

If you want to monetize your LinkedIn, read this:

Last year, I decided to test growing my newsletter.

It became my number 1 conversion platform.

Here's how I went from 0 to 30k+ subscribers:

I used highly intentional lead magnets.

1. Created a specific resource for people to use.
↳ My first one was a 30 page LinkedIn Playbook

2. Wrote an outstanding post to promote it.
↳ Good hook, great lead and some value in it.

3. Used FOMO to incentivize conversion.
↳ "Usually $197 but today its free"

4. Broke down exactly what they'd get.
↳ Used bulletpoints to explain quickly

5. Created a landing page opt-in to download it
↳ People could take action straight away.

This led to 5k+ subscribers in 42hrs.

LinkedIn is my number 1 attention platform. 
But newsletter is where conversion is happening.

Stop relying on 1 platform to grow your business.

LinkedIn + Newsletter is the million-dollar convo.

Start growing your newsletter today.

Repost ♻️ this if you want more tutorials like this.

PS: I made a free 7-day LinkedIn course for you: https://lnkd.in/e2cCfZeh

Newsletter became number 1 conversion platform

Lead magnets with perceived high value drive conversions

sources.author: Aurimas Griciūnas
sources.text:
𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮 𝗣𝗶𝗽𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀 𝗶𝗻 𝗠𝗮𝗰𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗟𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗦𝘆𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺𝘀 can become complex and for a good reason 👇

It is critical to ensure Data Quality and Integrity upstream of ML Training and Inference Pipelines, trying to do that in the downstream systems will cause unavoidable failure when working at scale.

It is a good idea to start thinking about the quality of your data at the point of creation (the producers). This is where you can also start to utilise Data Contracts.

Example architecture for a production grade end-to-end data flow:

𝟭: Schema changes are implemented in version control, once approved - they are pushed to the Applications generating the Data, Databases holding the Data and a central Data Contract Registry.

[𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗮𝗻𝘁]: Ideally you should be enforcing a Data contract at this stage, when producing Data. Data Validation steps down the stream are Detection and Prevention mechanisms that don’t allow low quality data to reach downstream systems. There might be a significant delay before you can do those checks, causing irreversible corruption or loss of data.

Applications push generated Data to Kafka Topics:

𝟮: Events emitted directly by the Application Services.

👉 This also includes IoT Fleets and Website Activity Tracking.

𝟮.𝟭: Raw Data Topics for CDC streams.

𝟯: A Flink Application(s) consumes Data from Raw Data streams and validates it against schemas in the Contract Registry.
𝟰: Data that does not meet the contract is pushed to Dead Letter Topic.
𝟱: Data that meets the contract is pushed to Validated Data Topic.
𝟲: Data from the Validated Data Topic is pushed to object storage for additional Validation.
𝟳: On a schedule Data in the Object Storage is validated against additional SLAs in Data Contracts and is pushed to the Data Warehouse to be Transformed and Modeled for Analytical purposes.
𝟴: Modeled and Curated data is pushed to the Feature Store System for further Feature Engineering.
𝟴.𝟭: Real Time Features are ingested into the Feature Store directly from Validated Data Topic (5).

👉 Ensuring Data Quality here is complicated since checks against SLAs is hard to perform.

𝟵: High Quality Data is used in Machine Learning Training Pipelines.
𝟭𝟬: The same Data is used for Feature Serving in Inference.

Note: ML Systems are plagued by other Data related issues like Data and Concept Drifts. These are silent failures and while they can be monitored, we don’t include it in the Data Contract.

Let me know your thoughts! 👇

Data quality should be addressed at the point of creation by producers
Data Contracts should be enforced at the data production stage
Data Validation steps downstream are Detection and Prevention mechanisms
There might be significant delay before downstream checks can be performed, causing irreversible corruption or loss of data
ML Systems are plagued by Data and Concept Drifts, which are silent failures
Data and Concept Drifts can be monitored but are not included in Data Contracts
inferredBeliefs: Proactive data quality measures are more effective than reactive ones
Upstream architecture decisions significantly impact downstream ML system reliability
Data contracts are a foundational best practice for production ML systems
Multi-stage validation is necessary for handling data quality at scale
Prevention is preferable to detection in data quality management
sources.summary: Data quality and integrity must be enforced at the point of data creation using Data Contracts, with upstream validation preventing irreversible failures in production ML systems. A comprehensive end-to-end architecture using tools like Kafka, Flink, and Feature Stores ensures high-quality data flows through multiple validation stages before reaching ML training and inference pipelines.

sources.author: Aurimas Griciūnas
sources.text:
A simple way to explain 𝗔𝗜 𝗔𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗠𝗲𝗺𝗼𝗿𝘆.

In general, the memory for an agent is something that we provide via context in the prompt passed to LLM that helps the agent to better plan and react given past interactions or data not immediately available.

It is useful to group the memory into four types:

𝟭. Episodic - This type of memory contains past interactions and actions performed by the agent. After an action is taken, the application controlling the agent would store the action in some kind of persistent storage so that it can be retrieved later if needed. A good example would be using a vector Database to store semantic meaning of the interactions.
𝟮. Semantic - Any external information that is available to the agent and any knowledge the agent should have about itself. You can think of this as a context similar to one used in RAG applications. It can be internal knowledge only available to the agent or a grounding context to isolate part of the internet scale data for more accurate answers.
𝟯. Procedural - This is systemic information like the structure of the System Prompt, available tools, guardrails etc. It will usually be stored in Git, Prompt and Tool Registries.
𝟰. Occasionally, the agent application would pull information from long-term memory and store it locally if it is needed for the task at hand.
𝟱. All of the information pulled together from the long-term or stored in local memory is called short-term or working memory. Compiling all of it into a prompt will produce the prompt to be passed to the LLM and it will provide further actions to be taken by the system.

We usually label 1. - 3. as Long-Term memory and 5. as Short-Term memory.

A visual explanation of potential implementation details 👇

And that is it! The rest is all about how you architect the flow of your Agentic systems.

What do you think about memory in AI Agents?

sources.author: Philipp Schmid
sources.text:
Combine RAG with reasoning models like o1. Search-o1 modifies the RAG to work with reasoning models like OpenAI o1 or QwQ, outperforming traditional RAG systems by integrating retrieved information into the Chain of Thought! 👀

Implementation
1️⃣ Choose a Reasoning LLM, e.g. QwQ-32B
2️⃣ Implement a dynamic search mechanism, e.g. special tokens (<|begin_search_query|>)
3️⃣ Reason-in-Documents, Separate LLM call to analyze and refine retrieved information, based on previous reasoning step and user query
4️⃣ Inject “cleaned” knowledge back into the “hold” reasoning chain (<|begin_search_result|>)
5️⃣ Continues the chain of thought reasoning to solve user queries.

Insights
🚀 Search-o1 outperforms base models by 4.7% on complex reasoning tasks
🧠 Outperforms human experts in physics (68.7% vs 57.9%) and biology (69.5% vs 68.9%)
📊 Outperforms RAG with 29.6% higher accuracy on multi-hop questions
💡 Reason-in-Documents filters out noise and irrelevant information

Prompts: https://lnkd.in/eUigiQBx
Paper: https://lnkd.in/ea7pXERs
Github: https://lnkd.in/eEJ-DQ58
imageDescriptions: [Image: table]
directClaims: Search-o1 outperforms base models by 4.7% on complex reasoning tasks
Search-o1 outperforms human experts in physics (68.7% vs 57.9%) and biology (69.5% vs 68.9%)
Search-o1 outperforms RAG with 29.6% higher accuracy on multi-hop questions
Reason-in-Documents filters out noise and irrelevant information
Search-o1 integrates retrieved information into the Chain of Thought
inferredBeliefs: Reasoning models fundamentally change how RAG should be implemented
Traditional RAG systems are suboptimal for complex reasoning tasks
Dynamic search integration during reasoning is superior to static retrieval
Multi-step refinement of retrieved information improves accuracy
sources.summary: Search-o1 combines RAG with reasoning models by integrating dynamic search into the chain-of-thought process, achieving significant performance improvements over traditional RAG and base models on complex reasoning tasks.

directClaims: Agent memory is provided via context in the prompt passed to LLM to help agents plan and react given past interactions or data
Episodic memory contains past interactions and actions performed by the agent, typically stored in persistent storage like vector databases
Semantic memory includes external information available to the agent and knowledge about itself, similar to RAG context
Procedural memory contains systemic information like System Prompt structure, available tools, and guardrails, usually stored in Git and Registries
Short-term/working memory is compiled from long-term and local memory sources into a prompt for the LLM
Types 1-3 are labeled as Long-Term memory and type 5 as Short-Term memory

Clear categorization of memory types helps practitioners build better AI agents
Memory management requires thoughtful integration of persistent and ephemeral storage
sources.summary: A structured breakdown of four types of AI agent memory—episodic, semantic, procedural, and working memory—and how they function together in prompt context to improve agent planning and decision-making.

sources.author: Philipp Schmid
sources.text:
Combine RAG with reasoning models like o1. Search-o1 modifies the RAG to work with reasoning models like OpenAI o1 or QwQ, outperforming traditional RAG systems by integrating retrieved information into the Chain of Thought! 👀

Implementation
1️⃣ Choose a Reasoning LLM, e.g. QwQ-32B
2️⃣ Implement a dynamic search mechanism, e.g. special tokens (<|begin_search_query|>)
3️⃣ Reason-in-Documents, Separate LLM call to analyze and refine retrieved information, based on previous reasoning step and user query
4️⃣ Inject “cleaned” knowledge back into the “hold” reasoning chain (<|begin_search_result|>)
5️⃣ Continues the chain of thought reasoning to solve user queries.

Insights
🚀 Search-o1 outperforms base models by 4.7% on complex reasoning tasks
🧠 Outperforms human experts in physics (68.7% vs 57.9%) and biology (69.5% vs 68.9%)
📊 Outperforms RAG with 29.6% higher accuracy on multi-hop questions
💡 Reason-in-Documents filters out noise and irrelevant information

Prompts: https://lnkd.in/eUigiQBx
Paper: https://lnkd.in/ea7pXERs
Github: https://lnkd.in/eEJ-DQ58

Reason-in-Documents filters out noise and irrelevant information
inferredBeliefs: Reasoning models require specialized RAG modifications to be effective
Chain of Thought integration is superior to traditional retrieval-augmented approaches
Dynamic search mechanisms within reasoning chains improve performance on complex tasks
sources.summary: Search-o1 enhances RAG by integrating retrieved information into reasoning models' Chain of Thought, achieving 4.7% improvement over base models and 29.6% higher accuracy on multi-hop questions. The approach uses dynamic search mechanisms and document reasoning to filter noise and inject cleaned knowledge back into the reasoning process.

sources.author: Alex Wang
sources.text:
AI agents are not a type of robot, software, or simple automation.

AI agents are a broad category and aren’t limited to LLMs.

From reinforcement learning agents in robotics, computer vision agents for surveillance, to predictive maintenance agents in manufacturing...They’re all AI agents focused on achieving different goals.

AI agents are autonomous digital entities designed to make decisions, adapt, and take actions independently to achieve specific goals within a dynamic framework.

And they are not the same as Agentic AI or Agentic Workflows.

Think of AI agents as the autonomous 'doers' in a system.

Agentic AI is the overarching concept of AI behaving autonomously and goal-oriented, often involving multiple agents or agentic workflows.

Agentic workflows provide the adaptive structure that enables AI agents to achieve their goals.

In short, AI agents operate within agentic workflows, and Agentic AI refers to the entire approach of autonomous, goal-driven AI systems.

We’re going to see and work with more and more AI agents on a daily basis.

✨Also, this is a topic I’ve been especially interested in lately, and I’ve invited Prof. Jia Li for a deeper conversation about it. I’ll be sharing more details about the interview soon, it’s going to be an interesting one!
__________________

For more on AI and learning materials, please check my previous posts.

I share my learning journey here. Join me and let's grow together. Alex Wang

sources.author: Liz Donovan, MS, ACC
sources.text:
Don’t negotiate against yourself.

When you voice a vulnerable statement, you might want to jump in to fill the silence.

This leads to you working against yourself by offering more information than necessary and potentially backtracking on your request (though maybe not as extreme as this video…).

I had to train myself to be okay with the silence.

I practiced making a statement and waiting for a response.

At first, the silence felt excruciating. In reality, it wasn't that long and it allowed the other person to form their response.

Even after they answered, I would let silence hang, giving them the space to add more information. People tend to like to fill the silence, and that can be advantageous when you need information.

Silence is a powerful tool to use in any situation, but especially when you’re negotiating your salary. You need to leave space for the other party to give you information.

Learn to be ok with the silence.

I’m conducting a 🚀 free webinar Feb. 7th 🚀 on how to negotiate your salary, giving you a roadmap to go into the process with confidence. You can sign up at the link in the comments.

— 
👋 I'm Liz; a certified coach who empowers women to own their impact and get the recognition they deserve.

🔔 Follow for more lessons learned that you can apply to your own salary negotiation

♻️ Share with others you think would benefit from this content
directClaims: Voicing vulnerable statements and filling silence leads to working against yourself by offering more information than necessary
Silence allows the other person to form their response
People tend to like to fill the silence, which can be advantageous when you need information
Silence is especially powerful when negotiating salary
inferredBeliefs: Comfort with silence is a learnable skill
Salary negotiation requires strategic communication tactics
Self-advocacy benefits from disciplined communication practices
Information asymmetry favors those who listen and wait
sources.summary: Liz Donovan teaches that silence is a powerful negotiation tool—by resisting the urge to fill pauses, you allow the other party to provide more information and prevent self-sabotage during salary negotiations. She offers a free webinar to help women negotiate compensation with confidence.

sources.author: Patrick Knisely
sources.text:
Making a video game is hard!

As I claw my way to the finish line, I can't help but reflect a bit on the process of getting to where I am, how my expectations have shifted, and how proud I am of the game. It's not done yet, and one of the hardest parts has been realizing over and over again that I've still got more work to do-- that my self-imposed deadlines and goals continue to not be met.

I had hoped by the middle of this month to be at a certain point, but that doesn't seem likely to happen anymore. I am closer than I was to that goal when I set it, though, and I have been working hard at it.

I can't wait to share the final game when it's done, whenever exactly that may be.

In the meantime if you're curious, there is a demo available over on Steam: https://lnkd.in/gD6Qq9xG
imageDescriptions: [Image: graphical user interface]
directClaims: Making a video game is hard
Self-imposed deadlines and goals continue to not be met
A demo is available on Steam
inferredBeliefs: Game development requires managing expectations and iterative problem-solving
Pride in work comes from sustained effort despite setbacks
Transparency with audience about progress delays builds credibility
sources.summary: Patrick Knisely reflects on the challenges of game development, acknowledging missed deadlines while expressing pride in his progress and inviting interested parties to try the Steam demo.

sources.author: Pascal BORNET
sources.text:
Cutting-edge Nanorobot killed a cancer cell

The Nanorobot has a weapon hidden in a nanostructure and exposed only in the tumor microenvironment, sparing healthy cells.

“If you were to administer it as a drug, it would indiscriminately start killing cells in the body, which would not be good. To get around this problem, we have hidden the weapon inside a nanostructure built from DNA.” says Professor Björn Högberg at the Department of Medical Biochemistry and Biophysics, Karolinska Institutet

Here, innovation meets practical application, empowering us to lead and adapt in an ever-evolving technological landscape. Learn about the Three Competencies of the Future. Join the IRREPLACEABLE community and read the Book: https://zurl.co/Ql3J

Source: Brood AI

sources.author: Joshua Walton, PE, CWI
sources.text:
The team I coach made it to world finals!
Please support if you can!
https://lnkd.in/eXWSDCN4
imageDescriptions:
[Image: Could you build a tower from balsa and super glue, weighing only 18g (0.04 lbs!), and have it hold 
643 lbs!?
The team of 4th and 5th graders from Washington GT Magnet Elementary did just that!

They tested the structure to failure along with a creative performance and earned GOLD at State Finals!

The win earned them a spot in the World Finals competition in May, where they will compete against teams from nearly every state and over 25 other countries.

Odyssey of the Mind is a creative problem-solving competition started over 40 years ago. I competed for 9 years in school and this year ended an 18 year retirement, as a first time coach!

We are raising money to help pay for flights to Worlds (Iowa State University) as well as for room and board while at competition.

If you would be able to help in any amount, please click here to donate.

Thanks,
Josh
]

sources.author: Nicholas Nouri
sources.text:
𝐇𝐢𝐠𝐡-𝐕𝐨𝐥𝐭𝐚𝐠𝐞 𝐋𝐢𝐧𝐞 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐚𝐢𝐫𝐬 𝐝𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐛𝐲 𝐫𝐨𝐛𝐨𝐭𝐬 🚀

Traditionally, this task demanded highly trained professionals equipped with specialized gear to brave the dangers. Yet, the advent of robotics and drones is rewriting the narrative, ushering in a new era of safety and efficiency.

First off, it's crucial to grasp the inherent risks of repairing high-voltage lines. These are the arteries of our electrical grid, pulsing with powerful currents that can be lethal. The work involves heights, high voltage, and exposure to the elements, making it one of the most hazardous occupations.

𝐑𝐨𝐛𝐨𝐭𝐢𝐜'𝐬 𝐫𝐨𝐥𝐞

The core of this innovation lies in its ability to carry out repairs without human intervention in the direct danger zone. These robots are designed to navigate along high-voltage lines, performing diagnostics, identifying issues, and executing repairs. Drones, on the other hand, offer aerial support, providing real-time data, mapping, and even delivering tools or components to the robots on the lines.

𝐖𝐡𝐢𝐥𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐦𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐚𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐠𝐞 𝐢𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐮𝐛𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐫𝐞𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐢𝐧 𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐤 𝐭𝐨 𝐡𝐮𝐦𝐚𝐧 𝐥𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐬, 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐫𝐢𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐞 𝐞𝐟𝐟𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐥𝐝:

> Increased Efficiency: Robots can operate in conditions that would be challenging, if not impossible, for humans, such as during inclement weather or in remote locations.

> Cost Effectiveness: Reducing downtime for repairs and minimizing the need for large ground crews translates into significant savings.

> Precision and Reliability: Robots can perform tasks with a level of accuracy and consistency that surpasses human capability, thanks to advancements in AI and machine learning.

𝐇𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫, 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐭𝐞𝐜𝐡𝐧𝐨𝐥𝐨𝐠𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥 𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐩 𝐝𝐨𝐞𝐬 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐢𝐭𝐬 𝐡𝐮𝐫𝐝𝐥𝐞𝐬:

> Technical Limitations: The complexity of some repairs may still necessitate human intervention, either remotely or on-site.

> Initial Investment: The upfront cost of developing, deploying, and maintaining robotic systems is substantial.

> Regulatory and Safety Standards: Integrating robots into the existing framework requires navigating a maze of regulations and ensuring they meet stringent safety standards.

It's clear that the role of robotics in our lives is not just about convenience or efficiency; it's about safeguarding human life and dignity.

How do you see robotics shaping the future of workplace safety and beyond?

hashtag
#robotics 
hashtag
#innovation 
hashtag
#technology 
hashtag
#safety 
hashtag
#ai
directClaims: High-voltage line repairs traditionally demanded highly trained professionals with specialized gear
Robots can navigate along high-voltage lines, performing diagnostics, identifying issues, and executing repairs
Drones provide real-time data, mapping, and deliver tools or components to robots on lines
Robots can operate in conditions challenging or impossible for humans, such as inclement weather or remote locations
Reducing downtime for repairs and minimizing ground crews translates into significant savings
Robots perform tasks with accuracy and consistency surpassing human capability through AI and machine learning
Some repairs may still necessitate human intervention, remotely or on-site
Upfront cost of developing, deploying, and maintaining robotic systems is substantial
inferredBeliefs: Robotics represents a fundamental shift in workplace safety paradigm
Human safety and dignity should be prioritized over convenience
Technology integration requires careful balance between innovation and regulatory compliance
AI and machine learning are enabling capabilities that exceed human performance in precision tasks
Challenges to robotic adoption are surmountable despite current technical and financial barriers
sources.summary: Nicholas Nouri discusses how robotics and drones are transforming high-voltage line repairs by removing humans from dangerous work while improving efficiency, though implementation faces technical, financial, and regulatory challenges.

sources.text: Happy to share that I’m starting a new position as Senior Lecturer at the UMN Carlson School of Management! Thank you to my family, friends, professors, colleagues, and former classmates for making this journey back to where I graduated possible.
